rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIGRID ANITA GERDA BORCHERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Amend Order Determining Inheritance Rights
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. The Motion to Amend Order Determining Inheritance Rights filed by Petitioner W.C. Cox & Co. is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The motion is improperly brought under CCP section 473 subd. (d). That subsection of the statute is designed to correct clerical errors, not substantive mistakes. "The test which distinguishes clerical error from possible judicial error is simply whether the challenged portion of the judgment was entered inadvertently (which is clerical error) versus advertently (which might be judicial error, but is not clerical error). Unless the challenged portion of the judgment was entered inadvertently, it cannot be changed post judgment under the guise of correction of clerical error." (Tokio Marine & Fire Ins. Corp. v. W. Pac. Roofing Corp. (1999) 75 Cal. App. 4th 110, 117-18.) Here, the challenged portion of the October Order was not entered "inadvertently." It was entered intentionally and only months later did counsel discover it is legally incorrect. To change the Order's determination of who the Estate of Elsa Marie Puettcher Doerrie ought to be distributed to would be to change the original, intended judicial determination in the order. This cannot be done under CCP section 473(d). However, on its own motion, the Court deems the motion to be a request for relief under CCP section 473(b). The motion appears to be based on counsel's mistake or excusable neglect with respect to German probate law, and relief is available under CCP section 473(b). Accordingly, the Court will set aside the Order Determining Inheritance Rights issued on October 7, 2020. The Court DENIES the request to distribute to the children of the post-deceased heir as requested. Counsel is free to file a new heirship petition requesting this relief. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on May 4, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. 1) None of the four Corrected I&As filed on 10/6/20 was properly verified by the sole co-executor who signed them and they were not signed by the other co-executor. This issue was noted in the tentative ruling on 3/23/21 and still has not been addressed. The Court may consider removing the co-executor(s). [Prob. C. Sections 8804,8852(b), and 11050.] 2) The accounting period is reportedly 1/5/17 - 1/4/19, but some of the schedules show the ending date as 2/28/19. 3) Only one page of the Chase bank statement is attached to the petition, not a complete statement. It is for the period ending 1/31/19 (not 1/4/19 or 2/28/19) and shows a balance in the checking account as $904,896.82 and in the savings account as $250,228.11. The accounting shows assets on hand as of 2/28/19, including the Chase checking account balance of $903,557.93 and savings account balance of $250,245.35. No reconciliation is shown. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 4) Robert Noelke's declaration filed on 3/9/21 regarding the original statements is still incomplete. [LRSF 14.60 D.] 5) The accounting shows many legal fees paid to Sharma Law Group totaling over $26,000 without any explanation or Court authority. 6) Several receipts and disbursements are not fully described. [Prob. C. Section 1062(a) and (b).] 7) There was over $903,000 in one checking account. [Prob. C. Sections 9700 and 46.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GAUALOFA M. CASEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/2021. 1) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. (CRC 7.53). 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/17/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS P WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/2/2021. 1) When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705 (b)]. 2) Please file receipts for all entries in the $1,477.84 and $32,939.91 requests for reimbursement. 3) Please explain and itemize the $1,500.00 in costs from FCF. 3) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/17/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA VALENTINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution, Settling First Account, Partial Allowance Of Administrators Commission And Costs And For Partial Allowance Of Ordinary Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The Settlement Agreement says $280,929.54 is to be distributed to Patricia's Estate on or before 9/28/2020. It is unclear when the estate received the $280,929.54. 2) Leland Valentine was appointed Administrator after a hearing with $300,000.00 bond. The I&A's allege assets of $1,132,939.92. See LRSF 14.16 A. Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond and LRSF 14.11 B. Examiner requested legal authority for not applying for an increase in bond. In response petition alleges an application to increase bond cannot be made because the parties must comply with the 02/18/2021 Court Order approving the Settlement. 3) Avid Eli Alavi has not been appointed as a GAL in this estate. Avid Eli Alavi was appointed as in the related trust for the benefit of David Valentine, Jr. Petitioner requests $354,506.00 be distributed to Avid, Eli Alavi, GAL for the benefit of David Valentine, Jr. Petitioner has not filed an amendment to change the distribution and instead appears has tried to amend the distribution in the Supplemental Declarations. 4) It appeared Administrator has kept assets over $250k limit in a Schwab account. In response Petitioner alleges the Schwab account is a sweep account that distributes the funds into Schwab-Affiliated Banks that are FDIC insured up to $250,000.00. The Bank Statement provided dated 09/01/2020 - 09/30/2020 shows $594,764.85 in Charles Schwab Bank and $249,000.00 in Schwab Premier Bank. 5) Petitioner requests a $10,000.04 reserve.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORMA GALIANO 1987 TRUST

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN A. CLEMENTS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/25/21. Item 3d(7) of the petition is incomplete. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Whose children are the nephews and niece? When the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, all trust beneficiaries must be listed in item 8 of the petition and must be clearly identified as such. Notice was mailed to Evan Eitner Clements and to Evan E. Clements at addresses that differ from the ones shown in item 8 of the petition The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Item 3c of the petition shows the decedent's residence at the time of death as 2021 Castro Street? Courtesy copies of the supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, and either a corrected proof of service or waiver of notice (or corrected item 8) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/14/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH FLOYD DEROUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/2/2021. 1) Petitioners' information must be included on page 4 under #8. 2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C. 3)] 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Petitioner has not filed proof of publication. 5) Petitioner has not filed notice of hearing DE-121. 6) Please provide legal authority for JoAnn Jones having priority for letters of administration. 7) Please explain how the $914,949.00 in personal property has no income. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/17/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
